used them to access the AWS API from an intermediate host
the actor created several instances in our infrastructure to do reconnaissance

attack_chain.description: Threat actor obtained a set of AWS keys and used them to authenticate to the AWS API from an intermediate host at another smaller US service provider.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a threat actor obtained access to a set of AWS keys and used them to access the AWS API from an intermediate host with another, smaller service provider in the US

evidence_chunks.quote: a threat actor obtained access to a set of AWS keys and used them to access the AWS API from an intermediate host with another, smaller service provider in the US

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Through the AWS API, the actor created several EC2 instances in OneLogin's infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Through the AWS API, the actor created several instances in our infrastructure

evidence_chunks.quote: Through the AWS API, the actor created several instances in our infrastructure
Through the AWS API, the actor created several instances in our infrastructure

attack_chain.description: Used the created instances to perform reconnaissance of the AWS environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: created several instances in our infrastructure to do reconnaissance

evidence_chunks.quote: created several instances in our infrastructure to do reconnaissance
created several instances in our infrastructure to do reconnaissance

attack_chain.description: Accessed database tables containing information about users, apps, and various types of keys (some data encrypted at rest).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor was able to access database tables that contain information about users, apps, and various types of keys

evidence_chunks.quote: The threat actor was able to access database tables that contain information about users, apps, and various types of keys
The threat actor was able to access database tables that contain information about users, apps, and various types of keys

attack_chain.description: Potential exfiltration of database contents; OneLogin could not rule out that the actor also obtained the ability to decrypt encrypted data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: at this time we cannot rule out the possibility that the threat actor also obtained the ability to decrypt data

evidence_chunks.quote: at this time we cannot rule out the possibility that the threat actor also obtained the ability to decrypt data
at this time we cannot rule out the possibility that the threat actor also obtained the ability to decrypt data

initial_access_vector: Compromised AWS API keys used from an intermediate host at another US service provider to authenticate to the AWS API.
persistence_mechanism: None explicitly described; attacker spun up EC2 instances using valid AWS keys but session was cut short by OneLogin revoking keys and terminating instances.
privilege_escalation: No explicit privilege escalation described; the stolen AWS keys already provided sufficient privileges to create EC2 instances and access databases.
